L1.area: Array and collection data transformation utilities

L2.description: A use-after-free (double free) vulnerability exists where a user-provided mapping callback that panics during array element transformation causes a single object to be dropped twice, because the original array is not guarded against automatic cleanup after elements have been moved out via unsafe pointer reads.

L3.area: Array element mapping and conversion logic using unsafe memory operations
L3.description: A use-after-free (double free) vulnerability exists in the array mapping routine that transforms elements using a user-provided callback. When the callback panics, elements that were already moved out via unsafe pointer reads are dropped a second time because the source array is not wrapped in a guard that prevents its automatic destructor from running.

ground_truth.reason: The map_array function uses unsafe ptr::read to move elements out of the source array and ptr::forget to prevent the array's destructor from running, but if the user-provided callback panics, forget is never called and the array's destructor double-drops already-moved elements.
